---
toc: false
sidebar: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-inner}

[BER 640 · Fall 2026]{.hero-eyebrow}

# Advanced Statistical Methods in Education {.hero-title}

[A Regression Approach with R]{.hero-subtitle}

[A graduate-level, R-driven tour of regression analysis for education researchers — from the simple linear model through ANOVA and logistic regression to the unifying Generalized Linear Model.]{.hero-lead}

::: {.hero-cta}
[{{< fa file-lines >}} Syllabus](syllabus.qmd){.btn .btn-hero-primary role="button"}
[{{< fa calendar-days >}} Schedule](schedule.qmd){.btn .btn-hero-secondary role="button"}
[{{< fa book-open >}} Lecture Notes]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}){.btn .btn-hero-outline target="_blank" role="button"}
:::

[Graduate · {{< var course.days >}} · {{< var course.dates >}} · {{< var university.name >}}]{.hero-meta}

:::
:::

::: {.grid .course-details}

::: {.g-col-4}
### Instructor
- {{< fa user >}} [{{< var instructor.name >}}]({{< var instructor.url >}})
- {{< fa envelope >}} [{{< var instructor.email >}}](mailto:{{< var instructor.email >}})
- {{< fa building >}} {{< var instructor.office >}}
- {{< fa clock >}} {{< var instructor.office_hours >}}
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Course Details
- {{< fa calendar >}} {{< var course.semester >}}
- {{< fa calendar-days >}} {{< var course.dates >}}
- {{< fa location-dot >}} {{< var course.location >}}
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Course Materials
- {{< fa book >}} [Lecture Notes]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa laptop-code >}} [R Lab Book]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa clipboard-list >}} [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa database >}} [regdatasets Package]({{< var books.regdatasets >}}){target="_blank"}
:::

:::

---

## Course Overview

This course adopts a **"specific to general"** pedagogical approach, beginning with simple linear regression (a model most students have encountered) and progressively extending to more complex models. By the end of the semester, students will see that familiar tests like the t-test and ANOVA are special cases of the **Generalized Linear Model (GLM)**.

### Learning Arc

::: {.learning-arc-panel}

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 9
%%| fig-alt: "Course learning arc diagram showing four parts: Part I Foundations (SLR, ANCOVA/ANOVA), Part II Extensions (Multiple Regression, Interactions), Part III Model Quality (Diagnostics), Part IV Beyond Linear (Logistic Regression, GLM/Ordinal). Arrows show the progressive flow from simple to complex models."
flowchart TD
    subgraph P1["Part I: Foundations"]
        A["SLR"] --> B["ANCOVA/ANOVA"]
    end
    subgraph P2["Part II: Extensions"]
        C["Multiple Regression"] --> D["Interactions"]
    end
    subgraph P3["Part III: Model Quality"]
        E["Diagnostics"]
    end
    subgraph P4["Part IV: Beyond Linear"]
        F["Logistic Regression"] --> G["GLM/Ordinal"]
    end

    B --> C
    D --> E
    E --> F

    style A fill:#0B3D66,color:#fff,stroke:#1A2A3A,stroke-width:2px
    style B fill:#0B3D66,color:#fff,stroke:#1A2A3A,stroke-width:2px
    style C fill:#2C6E91,color:#fff,stroke:#1A2A3A,stroke-width:2px
    style D fill:#2C6E91,color:#fff,stroke:#1A2A3A,stroke-width:2px
    style E fill:#6B3E7A,color:#fff,stroke:#1A2A3A,stroke-width:2px
    style F fill:#A8431E,color:#fff,stroke:#1A2A3A,stroke-width:2px
    style G fill:#A8431E,color:#fff,stroke:#1A2A3A,stroke-width:2px

    style P1 fill:#E6EDF3,stroke:#0B3D66,stroke-width:2px,color:#1A2A3A
    style P2 fill:#E5EEF3,stroke:#2C6E91,stroke-width:2px,color:#1A2A3A
    style P3 fill:#EFEAF2,stroke:#6B3E7A,stroke-width:2px,color:#1A2A3A
    style P4 fill:#F6E9E3,stroke:#A8431E,stroke-width:2px,color:#1A2A3A
```

:::

The course progresses from foundational regression models (Part I) through extensions and diagnostics (Parts II--III) to categorical outcomes and the unifying GLM framework (Part IV).

### What You Will Learn

1. **Specify and interpret** regression models with continuous and categorical predictors
2. **Diagnose** model assumptions and identify influential observations
3. **Build** models using both confirmatory and exploratory strategies
4. **Extend** regression to binary and ordinal outcomes via logistic regression
5. **Unify** all course models within the Generalized Linear Model (GLM) framework
6. **Implement** all analyses in R with reproducible code

### Course Materials

All course materials are open-access and published on GitHub Pages:

| Material | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [**Lecture Notes**]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}){target="_blank"} | Primary textbook with theory, math, and R code (13 chapters) |
| [**R Lab Book**]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}){target="_blank"} | Hands-on R tutorials for each topic (13 labs) |
| [**Project Guide**]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} | Guidelines and examples for the two course projects |
| [**regdatasets**]({{< var books.regdatasets >}}){target="_blank"} | R package with 24 curated datasets |

::: {.cta-secondary}
[New to the course? Start here.]{.cta-secondary-title}

[{{< fa download >}} Set up R & RStudio](resource/r-setup.qmd){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}
[{{< fa play >}} Begin with Week 1](content/01-content.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary role="button"}
:::
